title: Fix the Grade Calculator

meeting_script.name: Priya Sharma
meeting_script.message: Academic team submitted a grade calculator yesterday and QA rejected it. Ananya from the client side wants to explain what went wrong.

meeting_script.name: Ananya Singh
meeting_script.message: Yes. The function is giving wrong grades for some students. We have a specific grading system and it is not being followed correctly.

meeting_script.name: Ananya Singh
meeting_script.message: Our grades are: A for 90 and above, B for 75 to 89, C for 60 to 74, D for 40 to 59, and F for below 40.

meeting_script.name: Rahul Mehta
meeting_script.message: And if a student has no scores recorded at all — empty list — it should return NA not crash the system.

meeting_script.name: Sneha Kapoor
meeting_script.message: Also — averages should be rounded down. 74.9 is a C not a B. We use floor rounding always.

meeting_script.name: Priya Sharma
meeting_script.message: The original prompt and the failed tests are on your desk. Fix the prompt precisely — add what is missing, do not rewrite everything.

original_broken_prompt: Calculate a student's final grade based on their scores.
broken_code_produced:
def get_grade(scores):
    if not scores:
        return 'NA'
    avg = sum(scores) / len(scores)
    if avg >= 90: return 'A'
    if avg >= 75: return 'B'
    if avg >= 60: return 'C'
    return 'F'
